title: Allow FROM to be overriden with from option
body:
These changes will allow the "FROM" statement in a Containerfile
to be overridden with a new `--from` option.  If I have this Dockerfile.fedora

```
FROM fedora
```

This command will instead build an alpine image:

```
# buildah bud --from=alpine -t tom -f ~/Dockerfile.fedora .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
Completed short name "alpine" with unqualified-search registries (origin: /etc/containers/registries.conf)
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 188c0c94c7c5 done
Copying config d6e46aa247 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: COMMIT tom
--> d6e46aa2470
d6e46aa2470df1d32034c6707c8041158b652f38d2a9ae3d7ad7e7532d22ebe0
```

Addresses: #2404

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan*, *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Tried to make the man page a bit more clear on the Build Time Variables and finally chased down the white space issues.

This seems to conflict with the way imagebuilder does it:  as a dedicated CLI option which overrides the base image for only the first stage.  This lets it avoid adding a new predefined ARG.  An API caller isn't going to know that we're doing adding an ARG, and I'm worried that this could be hard to troubleshoot later on.
@nalind Do you have an alternative idea?

Instead of tunneling it through an ARG, make it a field in BuildOptions that's controlled by a CLI flag, and when we alter the parsed tree in `imagebuildah.Executor.Build()`, only do so if it's the first stage.
@rhatdan @nalind reworked and repushed.  Ready for the next round of reviews.  I've changed from using a Build Time variable passed in with a build-arg to creating a new `--from` option.
LGTM if you can get tests to pass.
--from needs to be added to completions.
@rhatdan, `--from` added to the completions and now this has all happy green test buttons.

I just ran the --from flag on buildah provided by registry.redhat.io/rhel8/buildah. The --from option is not there yet. 

Is there any idea when this option becomes available in registry.redhat.io/rhel8/buildah? 

Do I have an alternative?

I have attached the output of the buildah version and help commands:
[buildah-output.txt](https://github.com/containers/buildah/files/6378077/buildah-output.txt)

The feature landed in the v1.19 release.  The `quay.io/buildah/stable` image, which is built similarly, is currently at v1.19.8.  I expect an update to `registry.redhat.io/rhel8/buildah` for RHEL 8.4 will update it to something close to that version, depending on the timing of it.
Thank you nalind. It works when I use quay.io/buildah/stable (in a tekton task on openshift)! I intend to switch back to redhat once they have put it in.
@fatherlinux pinging you in case there's anything that needs to happen to update the Buildah image on the RHEL registries.
